purpose: Submit, inspect, retry, and cancel background jobs through a queue-backed orchestration boundary with explicit state tracking.

endpoints.description: Submit one job into a named queue.
Retrieve current job state, attempts, and timestamps.
Cancel a waiting or delayed job when cancellation is allowed.

dependencies.purpose: Queue-backed job lifecycle management with retries, delays, and inspection

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a workflow should return quickly and continue processing in background workers with explicit job states.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for synchronous request handling, durable workflow definitions, or direct shell execution without queue state tracking.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) POST /jobs with queue, name, and payload. 2) Poll GET /jobs/{job_id}. 3) Retry or cancel using explicit job endpoints when needed.

failure_modes.symptom: 503 error on job submission or status retrieval
failure_modes.recovery: Restore queue connectivity and retry submission

failure_modes.condition: Retry requested for a non-failed job
failure_modes.recovery: Only retry jobs currently in the failed state

allowed_hosts.purpose: Queue backend for BullMQ state and scheduling

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not enqueue jobs without explicit state tracking

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use BullMQ Queue, Job, and QueueEvents primitives. Expose only submission and state APIs here; worker execution belongs in a separate module. Enforce maximum attempts and safe backoff bounds.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Retries, delayed jobs, and rate limiting need a scheduler-aware BullMQ setup. Queue state can become confusing if cancellation is treated as deletion rather than explicit cancellation metadata.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep this module focused on queue lifecycle operations and represent state transitions explicitly in API responses.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not execute jobs inline in the API process and do not hide failed job reasons.
